2606.09520.title: Closing the Prior-Posterior Loop: Self-Reflective Molecular Design with Analysis-Driven LLM Iteration
2606.09520.paper_summary: Can a general-purpose large language model design molecules with the precision of a seasoned chemist? Current LLM-based frameworks answer this question with scalar feedback loops-generate, score, reject-that amount to informed trial-and-error. Here we show that replacing a single number with the full physicochemical rationale from first-principles calculations transforms the LLM from a stochastic sampler into a causal reasoner. Our system couples retrieval-augmented generation with a self-reflection module that feeds orbital energies, atomic charges, and electron densities-rather than compressed scores-back into the design loop. On HOMO-LUMO gap targets from 1.0 to 5.0 eV, this structure-property-relationship (SPR) reflection achieves a deviation as low as 0.0003 eV and a 100% success rate on moderate tasks, decisively outperforming scalar-feedback and non-reflective baselines. The framework generalizes seamlessly to dipole-moment design and proves robust across five distinct LLM backbones. These results establish a new paradigm: when the model understands not only that a molecule fails, but why, iterative molecular design becomes genuinely mechanistic.
2606.09520.paper_summary_zh: 一般用途的大型語言模型能否以資深化學家的精確度設計分子？目前基於LLM的框架通過標量反饋循環——生成、評分、拒絕——來回答這個問題，這相當於知情的試錯。在這裡，我們展示了用來自第一原則計算的完整物理化學原理替換單一數字，使得LLM從隨機抽樣器轉變為因果推理者。我們的系統將檢索增強生成與自我反思模塊結合，將軌道能量、原子電荷和電子密度——而不是壓縮的分數——反饋回設計循環。在HOMO-LUMO間隙目標從1.0到5.0 eV的範圍內，這種結構-性質-關係（SPR）反思達到了低至0.0003 eV的偏差，並在中等任務上實現了100%的成功率，明顯超越了標量反饋和非反思基準。該框架無縫地推廣到偶極矩設計，並在五個不同的LLM骨幹上證明了其穩健性。這些結果建立了一種新的範式：當模型不僅理解分子失敗的事實，還理解失敗的原因時，迭代的分子設計便變得真正具機械性。
2606.09520.author: Junyi Gong et.al.
2606.09520.authors: Junyi Gong, Zijie Qiu, Ben Zhong Tang
